100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 16, 1926, the British Empire faced a significant crisis as the General Council of the Trades Union Congress called for a nationwide general strike in support of miners, amid ongoing labor disputes. In the U.S., President Calvin Coolidge reaffirmed his administration's commitment to economic stability, emphasizing a pro-business agenda. Meanwhile, the world of sports was abuzz as the 1926 U.S. Open golf championship unfolded, drawing considerable attention from fans across the nation.
